api/client/python/openmeter/models.purpose: Public re-export facade for all generated Python SDK model types (dataclasses and enums). Delegates entirely to _generated/models — no hand-written model code lives here.

patterns.name: Wildcard re-export from generated layer
patterns.description: Re-export `_models` and `_enums` via `from .._generated.models._models import *` and `from .._generated.models._enums import *`. Never define or duplicate types here.

patterns.name: Optional _patch overlay
patterns.description: Try-import `_generated.models._patch` and merge its `__all__` into the module's `__all__`. Patch symbols extend (not replace) the generated set.

patterns.name: TYPE_CHECKING guard for patch stubs
patterns.description: Import `_patch` under `if TYPE_CHECKING:` for IDE type-narrowing without runtime cost.

patterns.name: Call patch_sdk() at module load
patterns.description: Always call `_patch_sdk()` after all re-exports to apply any monkey-patches from the generated layer.

patterns.name: __all__ propagation
patterns.description: Set `__all__ = __all__` from the generated module, then extend with patch symbols so consumers get a complete, non-redundant export list.
patterns.example:
__all__ = __all__
__all__.extend([p for p in _patch_all if p not in __all__])

key_file_guides.role: Sole file in the package; thin facade that merges generated models + patch overrides into one importable namespace.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add hand-written model classes here; they will be clobbered on the next `make gen-api` run.

anti_patterns: Defining or modifying model classes directly in this package instead of using the _patch mechanism
Removing the _patch_sdk() call — patch hooks will silently not apply
Manually maintaining __all__ instead of deriving it from the generated module

decisions.decision: Thin facade over generated code rather than duplicating types
decisions.rationale: All model types are generated from TypeSpec via `make gen-api`; any hand-written copy would drift immediately. The facade gives a stable import path (openmeter.models.*) regardless of generator internals.

api/client/python/openmeter/operations.purpose: Public re-export facade for all generated Python SDK operation classes (API call builders). Mirrors the same pattern as openmeter/models but for _generated/operations.

patterns.name: Wildcard re-export from generated operations
patterns.description: Re-export `_operations` via `from .._generated.operations._operations import *`. Never define operation methods here.

patterns.name: Optional _patch overlay
patterns.description: Try-import `_generated.operations._patch` and merge its `__all__`. Patch symbols extend the generated set non-destructively.

patterns.name: TYPE_CHECKING guard for patch stubs
patterns.description: Import `_patch` under `if TYPE_CHECKING:` for IDE support only.

patterns.name: __all__ propagation
patterns.description: Derive `__all__` from the generated module and extend with patch-only symbols.
patterns.example:
__all__ = __all__
__all__.extend([p for p in _patch_all if p not in __all__])

key_file_guides.role: Sole file; thin facade merging generated operation classes + patch overrides into one importable namespace.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not implement API call logic here; operations are generated. Adding code here breaks on next `make gen-api`.

anti_patterns: Implementing operation/request logic directly in this package
Manually curating __all__ instead of delegating to the generated module

decisions.decision: Stable public import path via facade over generated internals
decisions.rationale: Generated operation classes live under _generated and may be reorganised by the generator; the facade decouples consumer imports from generator layout.
